semanticOwner: TaxKit product owner with the apps/docs public-doc owner
evidence: The source receipt records that apps/docs/navigation.json and all 61 tracked public MDX files declare draft.
apps/docs/navigation.schema.json and @taxkit/docs-content validate status representation while the owner policy owns lifecycle consequences.
docs/README.md separates public docs from maintainer-document lifecycle policy.
rejectedAlternatives: Treat draft as proof that the public site is unpublished or unavailable; local content metadata cannot establish external availability.
Treat draft as accepted current public truth.
Relabel public MDX or navigation by bulk inference rather than explicit page-level acceptance.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: draft means authored, locally renderable, visibly labelled candidate public documentation. It is not accepted-current truth, publication, deployment, external availability, correctness, or user visibility. published means explicitly accepted current public documentation; it still does not prove runtime or external availability. Preserve the existing 61 public MDX files and navigation as draft unless repository evidence records explicit page-level acceptance.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: Public lifecycle semantics do not establish public availability.
Journey proof cannot treat draft metadata as public-runtime proof.
Automation may validate status representation and ownership, not external availability.

authorityImpact: The TaxKit product owner and apps/docs public-doc owner accepted these local lifecycle semantics. This decision grants no public-site, provider, publication, deployment, or external-readback authority.
nonClaims: Draft metadata does not prove public publication, unpublication, runtime availability, correctness, or user visibility.
This decision does not relabel any public MDX, navigation, generated source, or docs runtime.
carryingCost: Keep page-level acceptance evidence explicit before transitioning any draft page to published.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: A page-level acceptance record authorizes a specific draft-to-published transition; external availability still requires boundary-matched runtime evidence.
